steps.title: Separate the sentence cleanly
steps.content: The first clause explains that the Suzuki method is rooted in several central tenets. The next idea must begin a new sentence: a participial phrase, "Developed by Japanese violinist Shinichi Suzuki," modifies "the method," the subject of the following clause.

steps.title: Avoid comma-splice options
steps.content: Choices B and D keep the material in one sentence, leaving the independent clause about the method joined to the prior clause only by a comma. Choice C adds an awkward "that," and still fails to create the needed sentence break. The period cleanly separates the two structures. The answer is A.
